name.doc: App display name in headers + about screen

tagline.text: Your neighbourhood, your tasks, your files — one chat.

placeholder.text: Type a slash command or message…

active_thread.doc: a11y label on the active-thread title

thinking.doc: Placeholder shown while a dispatched skill is in flight

unknown_input.doc: Bot reply when parseInput returns kind:'unknown' (non-slash or unmatched slash)

dispatch_error.doc: Generic fallback when resolveDispatch returns an error shape

list_summary.doc: Header for list replies (until step 2 ships per-item inline keyboards)

canopy_chat_op_pending.text: /{{opId}} is a chat-shell command — not wired on mobile yet.
canopy_chat_op_pending.doc: Shown when the user invokes a canopy-chat-host op (/help, /threads, /settings, …) that webMain routes via localBuiltins; the RN port is a later #253 sub-step

list_empty.doc: Shown inside a list-kind bubble when items is empty

button_tap.doc: Synthetic user-bubble shown when a row button is tapped (#253 step 2 — label is the button label, item the targeted item id)

followup_prompt.doc: Generic fallback prompt when a row-tap dispatch returns needsForm and the op doesn't have an op-specific entry in pickPromptKey (#253 step 4)

followup_prompt_respond_to_item_body.doc: Friendlier prompt for [Help with] on a stoop post (respondToItem.body) — picked via pickPromptKey in src/core/followUp.js

form_title.doc: Title at the top of the multi-field form bubble (#253 step 6)

form_submit.doc: Submit button on the multi-field form bubble

form_label_body.doc: Label for the 'body' field — used by respondToItem etc.

form_label_when.doc: Label for the 'when' field — calendar/availability ops

download_not_wired.text: This file has no inline bytes to save — file id: {{fileId}}
download_not_wired.doc: [Download] tapped but the rendered row carries no embed.snapshot (#266)

embed_unnamed_file.doc: Fallback title for a file-card embed with no name (#270)

embed_untitled_event.doc: Fallback title for a time-card embed with no title (#270)

download_saving.doc: [Download] in flight — expo-file-system writeAsStringAsync is running (#266)

download_saved.text: ✓ Saved {{name}} to the app's Documents folder.
download_saved.doc: [Download] succeeded.  Was previously showing the file:// uri which read as garbage to users (A9 real-device follow-up 2026-05-27)

brief_empty.doc: Fallback when /brief has no sections to show (A3 real-device follow-up 2026-05-27)

record_empty.doc: Fallback when a record-shape reply has no fields (C2 follow-up 2026-05-27)

dm_send_failed.doc: Free-text in a DM thread failed to send via NKN

scan_subheading.text: Point the camera at a stoop-contact:// or stoop-invite:// QR. You can also paste it.

scan_perm_rationale.text: canopy-chat needs camera access to scan QR codes.

scan_grant_perm.doc: Grant-permission button label on QR scanner

scan_paste_link.doc: Toggle for paste fallback on QR scanner

scan_paste_placeholder.text: Paste a stoop-contact:// or stoop-invite:// URL

scan_unknown.text: Unrecognised QR. Expected stoop-contact:// or stoop-invite://.

scan_contact_added.doc: Bot bubble after stoop-contact:// scan + addContactFromQr succeeds

scan_invite_open.text: Opening join-buurt wizard with scanned invite…
scan_invite_open.doc: Bot bubble before opening joinGroup wizard with pre-filled invite

open.doc: E1 — file-reply card: open the file URL in a new tab.

unnamed.doc: E1 — file-reply card fallback name when none is given.

openInFull.doc: E5 — record/mini-page bubble: open the card in a full-height detail modal.

header_offer_of_help.text: 📩 Offer of help on your post
header_offer_of_help.doc: Header of the responder-card bubble (inbound help-with-response, #271)

btn_counter.doc: [Counter] button on responder card — opens an inline counter-offer prompt

accepting.doc: Pending bubble while acceptResponder is in flight

accepted.doc: Bot bubble after Accept succeeds + help-with-accepted envelope sent

accept_failed.doc: Bot bubble when acceptResponder substrate call rejects

declined.doc: Bot bubble after Decline (no substrate call; fires help-with-declined envelope)

counter_prompt.text: Reply with a counter-offer in this DM.
counter_prompt.doc: Inline prompt bubble shown after [Counter] tap on responder-card (#271)

chat.doc: Bottom-tab label for the main chat view

threads.doc: Bottom-tab label for the thread sidebar

settings.doc: Bottom-tab label for the settings screen

drawer_title.doc: Title at the top of the thread-drawer modal (#253 step 5)

drawer_open_a11y.doc: a11y label for the hamburger button that opens the drawer

new_placeholder.doc: Placeholder in the create-thread TextInput inside the drawer

message_count.doc: Per-thread message count shown under the name in the drawer list

help_with_thread_name.doc: Default name for the spawned thread when [Help with] is tapped (#253 step 7)

dm_thread_name.doc: Default name for the spawned thread when [Start DM] is tapped (#253 step 7)

loading.doc: Shown while the in-process bundle composes

boot_failed.doc: Error banner when agentBundle throws; {{message}} interpolated

transport_ready.doc: Shown when NknTransport completes its initial connect

agent_wiring_failed.doc: Shown when createRealHouseholdAgent throws on V1 boot

shell_stub_intro.doc: Placeholder body until ChatScreen renders real content

fab_a11y.doc: Accessibility label for the bottom-right slash FAB (#241)

modal_placeholder.doc: Placeholder shown inside the slash-FAB's TextInput when empty

not_available.text: QR scanner not available on this device.
not_available.doc: Defensive — localBuiltins fired but openQrScanner wasn't wired

opening.doc: Returned to chat when /scan-qr fires; the modal then takes over

panel_subtitle.doc: Subtitle under the title showing event count

panel_empty.doc: Empty-state when EventLog has no entries

no_log.text: Logs are not available on this device yet.
no_log.doc: Returned by localBuiltins.logs when eventLog dep is missing

empty.doc: Returned by localBuiltins.logs when query returns 0 events

heading.doc: Heading shown above an inline /logs list result

panel_opened.doc: Echoed back when a bare /logs opens the side-panel

row_ago.doc: Per-row staleness badge on list items (renderer.js → formatLastSync; {{ago}} comes pre-formatted as '2h' / '30m' / etc. via relativeAgo)

saved_locally.doc: Decentralized sync hint when 0 peers are reachable yet

synced_to.doc: Decentralized sync hint — N of M peers confirmed

pending.doc: Decentralized sync hint — count of peers not yet confirmed

unreachable.doc: Decentralized sync hint — count + comma-separated WebIDs

podless_empty.doc: Pod-less sync hint — empty lastSeen map

podless.doc: Pod-less sync hint — count + relative-ago of oldest peer

consequences.doc: N2 — ⓘ tooltip/aria label on a radio option that reveals its consequence.

step_raise.doc: Step-1 label in dispute wizard stepper (#258)

raise_intro.text: Describe what happened. This goes to the buurt; admins and (if your conflict policy is mediation) two random members see it.

about_post_label.doc: Header for the context card showing the disputed post

about_post_optional.doc: Field label when no postId pre-filled

summary_placeholder.text: Be specific. Avoid naming third parties unless necessary.

propose_intro.text: What would resolve this for you? Even if you're not sure, write what would feel "good enough" so the mediator has a starting point.

proposal_placeholder.text: e.g. "an apology + agreement not to use my tools without asking"

file_intro.text: Review + confirm. Filed disputes are visible to admins (and mediators if you picked mediation). You can withdraw via /leave-group → re-join, or via a follow-up post.

substrate_warn.text: ⚠️ V0 substrate gap: this files as a stoop post with kind:dispute. Full mediation flow (raiseDispute → proposeResolution → acceptResolution) lands when stoop ships those skills.
substrate_warn.doc: Warning banner about the substrate gap

filing.doc: Activity indicator label on Step-3 while submit is in flight

filed_message.doc: Bot bubble shown when the dispatch returns from onDispatched

success_intro.text: Admins (and mediators if you picked mediation) will see it in the buurt feed.

title.doc: Header on the 5.9b-followup BIP39 restore screen.

lede.text: Type the 24 words you wrote down when you first set up the app. They'll bring back your identity and circles.

placeholder.doc: Sample text in the multi-line input.

wordCount.doc: Live word-count nudge under the input.

submit.doc: Primary CTA on the entry screen.

cancel.doc: Secondary CTA → returns to the welcome screen.

empty.text: Type your 24-word recovery phrase to continue.

wrong-length.text: A recovery phrase is 24 words. Check spaces and try again.

invalid.text: That doesn't look like a valid recovery phrase. Check the spelling of each word.

storage.text: Couldn't save your identity. Try again, or restart the app and reopen this screen.

kicker.doc: Small uppercase kicker above the title (board 3A · P6.9).

title.doc: Header on the first-run mnemonic display.

lede.text: Without these words you can't get back to your things if your phone is lost. We don't have them.
lede.doc: Helper text — explains why this matters.

unavailable.doc: Shown when the host hasn't been able to read the vault.

later.doc: Tertiary CTA — leaves the ack off so a future banner can re-nudge.

bulk_no_list.text: No recent list to apply “all” to — run a list command first.
bulk_no_list.doc: E2 — shown when `/<op> all` has no recent listing to fan out over.
